title: Adam Davy's Five Dreams about Edward II and Other Middle English Verse (Furnivall's 1878 selection)
summary: The repository artifact is the pinned CME XML for University of Michigan item AHA2711.0001.001, whose immediate verbal source is Frederick James Furnivall's 1878 EETS edition. The edition-defined selection is not one medieval work: it contains five intellectual roots, while its Life of St Alexius root prints four versions in six witness streams. Furnivall transcribed the Five Dreams, the first Alexius version, both reordered Old Testament History excerpts, the manuscript-continuous Fifteen Signs and Lamentacio, and the separate Conception of Mary prologue from Bodleian MS Laud Misc. 622; the other five Alexius streams come from Cotton Titus A XXVI, the Vernon Manuscript, Laud Misc. 108, Laud Misc. 463, and Trinity College Oxford MS 57. Complete IIIF bundles are cached for Vernon and Laud 108. Only selected non-target images are public for Laud 622 and Laud 463, and no complete public surrogate was found for Cotton or Trinity 57. Physical witnesses, edition, scan, catalog data, U-M encoding, and repository serialization remain distinct rights and access layers.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is Furnivall's bounded five-work selection, not a single medieval work, the whole printed volume, or the whole Laud 622 manuscript.
The Life of St Alexius is one abstract hagiographic work represented by four version branches and six complete witness streams in the edition.
The two Solomon passages are non-contiguous excerpts from one Old Testament History; the later-printed 64-line passage physically precedes the 296-line passage in Laud 622.
Lamentacio animarum is retained as a manuscript-continuous child of Fifteen Signs rather than promoted to an independent title-level work.
The Conception of Mary material is a separately identifiable prologue-only witness: 86 XML long-line elements corresponding to 172 printed short-line positions.
Furnivall attributes only the Five Dreams to Adam Davy. Jerome is neither normalized as author nor inherited from Internet Archive metadata.
No intermediary transcript entity is asserted: Furnivall acknowledges Horstmann's notice of multiple Alexius versions, but the exact path from the six manuscripts to his working copy is unresolved.
London is the edition's publication place only. Essex localization belongs to the Laud 622 scribal-language layer and is not a composition place for the five-work selection.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2711
entities.description: The checked-out XML artifact consumed by the repository publication workflow through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME source tree.
notes: The XML contains 529 LG, 4,741 L, 17 P, and 424 PB occurrences. Six L are quotations nested in editorial notes, leaving 4,735 intrinsic target-text L.
The 124 unique PB references and omission wrappers describe edition-carrier structure; they do not add intellectual works.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHA2711.0001.001
entities.description: The public Middle English Compendium digital text from which the repository XML derives.
notes: The public item labels the text public domain in the United States and applies the NoC-US rights statement.
The encoding follows Furnivall's editorial sequence, including his displacement of the earlier Solomon fragment to pages 96-98.

entities.label: Frederick James Furnivall's 1878 edition
alternate_labels: Adam Davy's Five Dreams about Edward II
entities.description: The immediate printed source of the U-M encoding. The target selection occupies printed pages 11-98 in a larger EETS volume.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Adam Davy's Five Dreams about Edward II; The Life of St. Alexius; Solomon's Book of Wisdom; St. Jeremie's Fifteen Tokens before Doomsday; and The Lamentation of Souls, Early English Text Society, Original Series 69 (London: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society, 1878), target texts pp. 11-98.
bibliographic.title: Adam Davy's Five Dreams about Edward II; The Life of St. Alexius; Solomon's Book of Wisdom; St. Jeremie's Fifteen Tokens before Doomsday; and The Lamentation of Souls
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 69
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

notes: Furnivall explicitly rejects extending Adam Davy's authorship across the whole volume.
His mention of a traditional Jerome attribution is not accepted as evidence that Jerome authored the Middle English Fifteen Signs text.
The cached PDF is the complete volume; the target boundary is printed pp. 11-98.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Furnivall's 1878 edition
entities.description: A complete public PDF scan of the edition with an accompanying Internet Archive metadata record.
notes: The Internet Archive metadata has no rights or license URL and incorrectly includes Jerome in its creator field; neither field is propagated as catalog authority.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Laud Misc. 622, selected target loci
entities.description: The principal manuscript behind the edition-defined selection: Dreams ff. 26vb-27va; Alexius version 1 ff. 21va-26vb; Old Testament material around f. 69; and Fifteen Signs, Lamentacio, and the Conception prologue on f. 71. The exact loci remain scoped to the individual components rather than flattened into one continuous target span.

holding.notes: The current catalog describes a composite manuscript in two parts. Furnivall's historical observation that the displaced first quire is now ff. 65-72 remains an edition-level claim.

notes: DIMEV localizes the relevant Laud 622 scribal language to Essex (LALME/eLALME LP 6260); this is witness-level evidence only.
The Old Testament History begins imperfectly, and Furnivall reports that the present ff. 65-72 quire was displaced to the end.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian single-slide object for MS. Laud Misc. 622
entities.description: The current IIIF object exposes one canvas only, f. 65r. That leaf belongs to the broader Old Testament History context but does not overlap the cataloged excerpts at ff. 69-71.
notes: Target-work presence in the cached manifest is absent; no work portion is asserted.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Titus A XXVI, ff. 145-153
entities.description: The witness of Furnivall's 422-line couplet version of the Life of St Alexius.

holding.notes: The component catalog groups the Life of St Alexis with other contents at ff. 143-207; DIMEV and Furnivall delimit the target to ff. 145-153.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Eng. poet. a. 1, ff. 44rb-45ra
entities.description: The Vernon witness of the 612-line earlier six-line-stanza Life of St Alexius version.

holding.notes: The current catalog gives the Alexius locus as ff. 44rb-45ra.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: The complete 710-canvas Digital Bodleian object; the target Alexius stream occupies ff. 44rb-45ra on canvases 105-107, with f. 45v retained as a checked end-boundary image.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Laud Misc. 108, ff. 233v-237ra
entities.description: The second 612-line witness stream of the earlier six-line-stanza Life of St Alexius version, in the codex's separately produced final quire.

date_statements.label: target manuscript unit
date_statements.notes: The current catalog assigns the final quire, ff. 229-237, to Scribe D and treats it as a separate unit.

holding.notes: The exact target locus is ff. 233v-237ra.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian facsimile of MS. Laud Misc. 108
entities.description: The complete 496-canvas Digital Bodleian object; the target Alexius stream occupies canvases 482-489, ff. 233v-237r.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Laud Misc. 463, ff. 116ra-118v
entities.description: The 635-line witness stream of the later six-line-stanza Life of St Alexius version.

holding.notes: DIMEV and Furnivall give the target locus as ff. 116ra-118v.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image from MS. Laud Misc. 463
entities.description: The current public IIIF object exposes only f. 115r. The target Alexius stream begins at f. 116ra, so the cached selected image has no target-text overlap.
entities.reproduction_format: One selected IIIF canvas at 1600-pixel width
notes: Target-work presence in the cached bundle is absent; no work portion is asserted.

entities.label: Trinity College Oxford MS 57, from f. 73
entities.description: The 636-line witness stream of the later six-line-stanza Life of St Alexius version. It begins at f. 73; Furnivall's XML-derived milestones run through f. 77v, but the exact current-catalog endpoint remains unverified.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: fourth quarter of the fourteenth century, after 1375

holding.notes: The current Gameson catalog PDF is publicly linked but returned an automated proof-of-work challenge during this retrieval.

entities.label: DIMEV 2.0-alpha.1 catalog dataset
entities.description: The complete 25 June 2026 DIMEV release used to identify the five work traditions, four Alexius version records, six witness streams, and the Conception prologue's separate status.
notes: The target records consulted are 379, 382, 3102, 4178, 4622, 4923, 5993, and 6345.
The cached object is a complete supporting dataset, not a copy containing the target literary work itself.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
entities.description: Official contextual guidance used only for the United States term analysis of Furnivall's 1878 edition; it is not part of the target work's textual transmission.
notes: The cached PDF is a complete supporting law/guidance source with target-work presence absent.

rights.component: Local XML serialization and inherited OCR/encoding layer
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository's Unlicense text is framed in software/source/binary terms and does not clearly establish that the legacy OCR/XML text layer was dedicated under those terms.
notes: Do not infer XML-layer rights from the public-domain status of Furnivall's edition.

rights.basis: The U-M item page expressly labels the item Public Domain and applies No Copyright - United States.

rights.component: Furnivall's 1878 editorial text and the exact medieval passages published in it
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1878, long before the current pre-1931 public-domain threshold stated in Copyright Office Circular 15A.

rights.component: Furnivall's editorial contribution and exact witness passages first made public in the 1878 edition
rights.basis: Furnivall died in 1910; the 1878 edition and its published-edition arrangement are outside their ordinary UK terms. The conclusion is scoped to the exact passages printed in 1878, not unprinted manuscript content or variants.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan, PDF packaging, and metadata layer
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive metadata record supplies neither a rights value nor a license URL.
notes: The underlying 1878 edition is public domain, but that does not itself license every scan-layer component.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as a material object
rights.basis: Physical ownership, custody, access, and reproduction permission are not the same as copyright status.
notes: The exact target witness passages printed in 1878 are covered by the scoped edition-rights analysis; unprinted variants and other manuscript contents are not assessed here.

rights.component: Bodleian manuscript photography and IIIF image layer
rights.basis: The Digital Bodleian manifest attributes the image to the Bodleian and applies CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as a material object
rights.basis: The catalog establishes custody and access conditions, not a copyright grant over the physical codex.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as a material object
rights.basis: Physical custody and access conditions are distinct from copyright.
The Digital Bodleian object applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to the images.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as a material object
rights.basis: Physical custody, reading-room access, and copying permission are distinct from copyright.

rights.component: DIMEV 2.0-alpha.1 catalog data and release files
rights.basis: The Zenodo release applies Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: Official U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A text and PDF
rights.basis: The circular is an official United States Government work; 17 U.S.C. 105 makes United States copyright protection unavailable for such works, subject to the section's exceptions.
notes: Its public-domain status does not confer rights in the works whose duration it helps researchers assess.

editorial_practices.description: Furnivall assembled five intellectual works and six Alexius witness streams in one edition-defined selection rather than presenting one medieval work.
The editor moved the earlier 64-line Solomon passage after the 296-line Book of Wisdom extract to complete his selected Life of Solomon sequence.
editorial_practices.scope: Old Testament History excerpts on pp. 82-90 and 96-98

editorial_practices.description: The edition juxtaposes four Life of St Alexius versions represented by six manuscript streams, including parallel texts for the two paired six-line versions.
editorial_practices.scope: Life of St Alexius on printed pp. 17-80
notes: This records the edition's comparative arrangement, not an unverified claim about the exact working-copy route for each witness.

editorial_practices.description: The encoding retains the title page and target texts while representing omitted front and back matter through structural placeholders and page-break scaffolding.

evidence.citation: Pinned CME source XML for aha2711.
evidence.locator: IDG AHA2711.0001.001; BODY divisions, headings, LG/L/P/PB structures, notes, and manuscript milestones
evidence.summary: Supplies the exact encoded selection and its structure. A fresh audit found 529 LG and 4,741 L, of which six L are editorial-note quotations and 4,735 belong intrinsically to the five target works.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Provides an Unlicense-form text framed around software and source/binary forms; its application to the inherited legacy OCR/XML layer is not explicit enough for a high-confidence rights claim.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Middle English Compendium item AHA2711.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Identifies the public digital item, title and publication data, and expressly states Public Domain with No Copyright - United States.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Adam Davy's Five Dreams about Edward II, EETS Original Series 69 (1878), complete cached PDF.
evidence.locator: title page; introduction; target texts pp. 11-98; editorial notices before the Solomon fragment and Lamentacio
evidence.summary: Establishes the immediate edition source, publication facts, witness labels, six-stream Alexius arrangement, Adam Davy attribution boundary, Solomon reordering, and manuscript continuity of Fifteen Signs with Lamentacio.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for adamdavysdreamsa00furnuoft.
evidence.summary: Identifies the scan and source files. Rights and licenseurl are null; the creator field's Jerome entry is not accepted as authorship authority.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, release 2.0-alpha.1, Zenodo record 20850921, 25 June 2026.
evidence.summary: Identifies the Five Dreams, four Alexius version records and six witnesses, Old Testament History tradition, Fifteen Signs record, and the separately transmitted Conception of Mary prologue; supplies manuscript loci and language localization.
notes: DIMEV's 318-line Furnivall extract count for Old Testament History conflicts with the XML's 296 plus 64 lines and remains open.
DIMEV's broader Conception work description gives an 84-line prologue, while this Laud copy has 86 XML long lines or 172 printed short-line positions.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Laud Misc. 622.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for shelfmark, composite two-part structure, parchment support, end-fourteenth-century date, relevant contents, and the fact that only one image from 35mm film is public.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian single-slide IIIF manifest for MS. Laud Misc. 622.
evidence.summary: The complete one-canvas bundle and visual inspection confirm that the public object contains a readable image of f. 65r only, with no overlap with Furnivall's bounded target loci at ff. 69-71; the manifest supplies CC BY-NC 4.0 image terms.
notes: The standalone provider manifest is also cached at source-cache/aha2711/bodleian-ms-laud-misc-622-single-slide-iiif-manifest.json with SHA-256 2f45a3dfac9c96162b8de4ff87f6754ecccaa536b7f9b2ac60469805230e411b.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Cotton MS Titus A XXVI component 041-001607032.
evidence.locator: Life of St Alexis and related contents, ff. 143-207
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark, English fifteenth-century component, physical-request route, and absence of digitized content; DIMEV and Furnivall refine the Alexius target to ff. 145-153.

evidence.locator: Life of St Alexius ff. 44rb-45ra; codex date and physical description
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for the Vernon shelfmark, late-fourteenth-century date, and exact Alexius locus.

evidence.locator: 710 canvases; target ff. 44rb-45ra, canvases 105-107; f. 45v boundary canvas 108 also checked
evidence.summary: Supplies the complete current-codex sequence, exact target canvas mapping, and CC BY-NC 4.0 image terms. Every bundled member hash and byte count was verified.

evidence.citation: Richard Gameson, Trinity College Oxford MS 57 catalog description (2018), official catalog PDF and Special Collections access page.
evidence.summary: Supplies the current date in the fourth quarter of the fourteenth century after 1375, parchment support, and repository access route. Automated retrieval of the PDF encountered a proof-of-work challenge, and no exact local file was cached.

evidence.citation: Local visual inspection of target-boundary images from the complete Vernon and Laud 108 IIIF bundles, 16 July 2026.
evidence.locator: Laud 622 f. 65r single canvas; Vernon canvases 105-108; Laud 108 canvases 482-489
evidence.summary: Confirmed the readable Laud 622 f. 65r image and its non-overlap with the target, plus readable target-boundary images and catalog-to-canvas mappings for the complete Vernon and Laud 108 facsimiles.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.summary: States the current U.S. public-domain threshold used for the 1878 edition analysis.

evidence.citation: United States Code, title 17, section 105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.summary: Official statutory text states that United States copyright protection is unavailable for United States Government works, subject to the section's specified exceptions; it supports only the rights status of the cached Copyright Office circular itself.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: duration of copyright (term).
evidence.summary: Supplies UK duration rules, including authorial, anonymous, unpublished-at-end-of-1988, and 25-year published-edition terms. The analysis therefore distinguishes exact passages published in 1878 from any unprinted manuscript content.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections mediated copying service.
evidence.summary: Provides the current mediated reproduction route and confirms that provider terms and permissions remain separate from public availability and copyright analysis.

open_questions.question: Did Furnivall transcribe every Alexius witness directly, or did one or more versions reach him through Horstmann transcripts or prior prints?
next_steps: Inspect Furnivall correspondence, EETS production papers, and Horstmann's relevant publications or transcripts.
Add an intermediary entity only if a specific documentary source establishes it.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current-catalog endpoint of the Trinity College Oxford MS 57 Alexius witness, and is a complete public facsimile available?
next_steps: Consult the Gameson catalog PDF interactively or request the relevant description from Trinity Special Collections.
Ask Trinity whether a complete digital surrogate or reproduction can be supplied.
notes: The witness starts at f. 73, and Furnivall-derived milestones run through f. 77v; only the exact endpoint is unresolved.

open_questions.question: Why does DIMEV describe Furnivall's Old Testament History extract as 318 lines when the XML has 296 plus 64 lines, totaling 360?
next_steps: Compare DIMEV's counting convention against the edition and Laud 622 line by line.
Check whether DIMEV excludes omitted, shared, or differently lineated passage segments.

open_questions.question: Does DIMEV record 4622 intend its Laud 622 witness to cover only the 22-line Fifteen Signs opening or the full 54-line manuscript-continuous Signs plus Lamentacio sequence?
next_steps: Compare the DIMEV witness locators and first/last-line data directly with Laud 622 and Furnivall pp. 92-96.
Retain the current explicit parent-child boundary unless stronger evidence requires revision.

open_questions.question: Can a complete digital surrogate of Laud Misc. 622, including ff. 21va-27va and 69-71, be obtained?
next_steps: Request target-locus reproductions through the Bodleian mediated copying service.
Recheck Digital Bodleian if the one-slide object is expanded.

open_questions.question: What rights analysis applies to manuscript variants and content not demonstrably published in Furnivall's 1878 edition?
next_steps: Keep reuse claims scoped to the exact passages published in 1878.
Obtain repository permission and specialist legal review before distributing unprinted variants or new photography.
